subject: [PATCH v5 0/2] mm: improve large folio readahead for exec memory

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a pre-existing issue in the decrement sites for mmap_miss, sent an independent patch to address it, and plans to send the next version of the series on top of this patch addressing other issues raised by sashiko.
sentiment_signals: plans to send the next version
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks Andrew! So sashiko seems to have brought out some interesting problems,
some of which are pre-existing. For example, the decrement sites for mmap_miss
in do_async_mmap_readahead() and filemap_map_pages() dont skip for VM_SEQ_READ.

I have sent this as an independent patch in [1].

I will send the next version of this series on top of [1] in a few days
addressing the rest of the issues raised by sashiko.

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260525145751.2671248-1-usama.arif@linux.dev/

2026-05-25.patch_summary: This patch improves large folio readahead performance for executable memory by addressing two issues in the do_sync_mmap_readahead() function. The first issue is that the mmap_miss heuristic, intended to throttle speculative readahead, also disables exec readahead when it exceeds a certain threshold. The second issue is that the force_thp_readahead path is gated only on specific conditions, preventing large folio readahead from being enabled even when supported by the mapping. The patch generalizes the gate and removes architecture-specific limitations, allowing larger folios to be read ahead for executable memory. This results in improved performance, with a 50% reduction in cold fault time and a 23% improvement in random access time on arm64 systems.
